channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Trump renews threat to destroy Iranian bridges and power plants if Strait of Hormuz is not reopened by Tuesday night
item.description: The president’s warning came as he briefed reporters at the White House on the daring mission to save two U.S. airmen whose fighter jet was shot down over Iran on Friday. He brushed off a question about whether bombing such infrastructure would violate the Geneva Conventions.

item.title: ICE arrests in D.C. region reach nearly 20,000 during Trump’s second term
item.description: Of the roughly 19,500 people arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement in D.C., Maryland and Virginia between Jan. 20, 2025, and March 10, 2026, about 11,600 — 60 percent — had no prior criminal record.

item.title: The Artemis II crew has flown farther from Earth than any humans before
item.description: The crew of the NASA mission has zoomed more than 248,655 miles from Earth in a historic voyage around the far side of the moon. The new distance record, set about 2 p.m. Eastern on Monday, surpasses a milestone set by Apollo 13 in 1970.

item.title: Virginians deeply divided on Spanberger months after her landslide win, poll finds
item.description: Democrat Abigail Spanberger won the race for Virginia governor by 15 points last year after touting her reputation for bipartisanship built on three terms representing a conservative-leaning district in Congress. But her approval rating stands at 47 percent two months into her term, a Washington Post-Schar School poll finds.

item.title: Supreme Court sides with Steve Bannon in efforts to dismiss his conviction for contempt of Congress
item.description: The former chief strategist to President Donald Trump spent four months in prison in 2024 after defying a congressional subpoena related to the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol. The justices vacated a judgment by an appeals court that upheld his conviction, sending the case back for reconsideration.

item.title: Schools are closing for the NFL draft. What does this teach students?
item.description: Pittsburgh prides itself on showing up and working hard. Students should, too.

item.title: He spent years working for Elon Musk. Here’s what this former Tesla president learned.
item.description: The billionaire’s demanding leadership style shaped one of the world’s most valuable companies. Jon McNeill shares how he survived.

item.title: Trump’s threat. Iran rescue. Moon mission. Secretive deals. March Madness. Dementia risk. Fart science. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: How Congress could put the Constitution in every American’s pocket
item.description: The Liberty Dollar Bill Act would be a step toward national unity, Randy Wright writes.

item.title: Surprising links between autism, Alzheimer’s could change how we treat both
item.description: The idea that two conditions at opposite ends of life might be biologically linked is beginning to upend long-standing assumptions in brain science, blurring a divide that has shaped the field.

item.title: What the looming sale of CNN means for Trump’s feud with the network
item.description: Animosity from the White House has taken on new meaning amid the network’s imminent sale to David Ellison’s Paramount Skydance, fueling anxiety among journalists.

item.title: Trump’s profane post threatening Iran’s power plants underscores tensions over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Gas prices have risen sharply as Iran limits the flow of oil through the Strait of Hormuz, ramping up the political pressure on President Donald Trump to end the conflict he started. His threat on Sunday heightens earlier warnings that he plans to inflict severe damage if there is no movement toward a deal.

item.title: We may have even less control over how long we live than previously thought
item.description: A study resets the longevity discussion, after previous research focused on those born between 1870 and 1900, and shows genetics and environment play roughly equal roles.

item.title: Easter destroys the ultimate weapon of tyranny
item.description: In proclaiming the resurrection of Jesus, Easter denies the power of death, Robert Barron, the bishop of the Diocese of Winona-Rochester and the founder of Word on Fire Catholic Ministries, writes.

item.title: Pope issues commanding call for peace as Trump, aides invoke God for war
item.description: “Let those who have weapons lay them down!” the first American pope declared, as the White House’s war in Iran and nativist agenda at home is testing the Vatican.

item.title: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now
item.description: Crossword and a coffee: Solve the Sunday puzzle now

item.title: In race to find downed U.S. airman, CIA launched a deception campaign to throw off Iran’s hunt, official says
item.description: The U.S. military and CIA coordinated on a daring mission that safely rescued from the Iranian mountains the missing crew member of a fighter jet that was shot down, bringing to a close a dramatic and politically perilous moment in the war.

item.title: U.S. rescues missing crew member in Iran after fighter jet shot down
item.description: The successful return of the missing airman, according to a U.S. official familiar with the operation, came after an intense search-and-rescue mission. While one crew member was quickly recovered after the Air Force F-15E jet was shot down Friday, the search continued into Saturday for the second.

item.title: Extreme saving hacks that are clever, unorthodox — and maybe a bit unethical
item.description: Readers share their most outrageous tips for saving money.

item.title: How a twangy country ballad captivated the nation
item.description: “Choosin’ Texas” — breakout Nashville singer Ella Langley’s wistful ode to lost love and two-stepping — is surpassing songs from pop superstars.

item.title: Aldi’s wine selection is surprisingly good. Here are 6 bargain bottles to try.
item.description: Plus, how to find gems among the grocery chain’s inexpensive, private-label wines.

item.title: Even if Iran war ends soon, Americans’ economic pain could linger for months
item.description: Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz is disrupting economic activity across Asia and Europe. The U.S. may escape the worst effects, but it will not emerge unscathed.

item.title: Have you heard the Hum? You might not even know it.
item.description: This mysterious sound turns Times Square into a work of art.

item.title: A Metrobus slammed into a restaurant in D.C.’s Shaw neighborhood, injuring three
item.description: Photos showed the bus halfway inside the Balkan restaurant Ambar, which officials said was unoccupied at the time of the Saturday morning crash.

item.title: He couldn’t think, eat or recover — and no one knew why
item.description: Doctors were stumped by an Oregon man’s deterioration. Then an infectious disease specialist had an aha moment.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.

item.title: Chinese firms are marketing detailed intelligence on movements of U.S. forces in Iran war
item.description: The private companies — some with links to the People’s Liberation Army — are marrying artificial intelligence with open-source data to market information they claim can “expose” the movements of U.S. forces. Beijing has sought to distance itself from any direct involvement in the Iran war.

item.title: How to deal with chronically negative people
item.description: You probably can’t turn your favorite pessimist into an optimist, but here’s what can actually help — and how to set boundaries so you don’t feel dragged down.

item.title: President Donald Trump weighs more Cabinet changes but wants to avoid a ‘massive shake-up,’ according to White House official
item.description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer are vulnerable, according to a White House official, who spoke on the condition of anonymity to discuss internal conversations. Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard appears more secure, the official said.

item.title: JD Vance’s new book features a photo of their church. They don’t know him.
item.description: The members of a tiny Methodist church in Virginia are excited — if a little confused — that the vice president’s memoir of his path to Catholicism has put them in the spotlight.

item.title: Correction: Two U.S. warplanes shot down; search ongoing in Iran for 1 missing crew member. An earlier alert said both planes were shot down in Iran.
item.description: An F-15 fighter jet and an A-10 attack plane were struck by hostile fire. Two Black Hawk helicopters also were hit, injuring the crews, before safely returning to their base, officials said.

item.title: A second U.S. warplane was shot down in Iran and its pilot rescued, officials said. One person is missing from an earlier crash.
item.description: An F-15 fighter jet and an A-10 attack plane were struck by hostile fire. Two Black Hawk helicopters also were hit, injuring the crews, before safely returning to their base, officials said.

item.title: Poll shows mixed signs for Democratic push to gain seats in Congress
item.description: A slight majority of Virginia voters supports giving Democrats a temporary edge in congressional maps, but opponents are more motivated to vote on the issue, according to a Post-Schar School poll.

item.title: Carolyn Hax chat: Is a dream job worth moving near my toxic mom?
item.description: Carolyn Hax chat: Is a dream job worth moving near my toxic mom?

item.title: One rescued after U.S. fighter jet crash in Iran, officials say, with intense search underway for second crew member
item.description: The condition of the rescued crew member was not immediately clear, the officials said.

item.title: We’re all noticing the wrong thing about the photographs of Kristi Noem’s husband, our columnist writes
item.description: Earlier this week, the Daily Mail published an exposé on Bryon Noem, the husband of the former homeland security secretary. Upon seeing the photos, the first word that came to mind was not “lurid,” columnist Monica Hesse writes. It was “lonely.”

item.title: U.S. fighter jet crashes in Iran, prompting search for 2 crew members, officials say
item.description: It’s the first known loss of an American aircraft inside Iranian territory since the war began a month ago.

item.title: The lesson of Pam Bondi’s firing
item.description: The lesson of Pam Bondi’s firing

item.title: 10 questions, and don't forget the bonus wager! Finish off your week with On the Record.
item.description: Think you’ve got what it takes to earn a perfect score? Take today’s longer news quiz and complete your point total for the week.

item.title: U.S. economy created 178,000 jobs in March, a strong gain for a vulnerable labor market
item.description: The unemployment rate edged down to 4.3 percent.

item.title: Pam Bondi. Pete Hegseth. Trump ballroom. ICE arrests. Artemis II. Triplet cyclones. Octopus sex. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Catastrophic sewage spill in Potomac River followed years of delay on repairs, Post review finds
item.description: An investigation by The Washington Post reveals that a prolonged environmental review pushed back work that was initially proposed in 2018. The pipe failed Jan. 19 and released one of the largest spills of untreated wastewater in U.S. history.

item.title: 7 things to know about the end of life, according to death doulas
item.description: They guide people through their final life transition. Here’s what they want everyone to know about dying.

item.title: Hegseth forces out Army’s top general in latest clash between Pentagon and military leadership
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asked Gen. Randy George to step down and retire, an extraordinary move amid the war with Iran.

item.title: Trump sets 100% drug tariffs on companies that haven’t lowered prices
item.description: Makers of patented pharmaceuticals can escape the import tax by agreeing to join President Donald Trump's “most favored nation” program to reduce U.S. drug prices or by establishing new factories to serve the American market, according to a senior administration official.

item.title: White House ballroom project approved by federal commission packed with Trump allies
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday ordered a halt to the project. The commission’s chair said the ruling didn’t affect the panel’s review.

item.title: Trump ousts loyalist Pam Bondi as attorney general
item.description: The president praised her tenure and said the Justice Department’s No. 2 official, Todd Blanche, will temporarily step into the role. The move comes weeks after Trump dismissed Kristi L. Noem as homeland security secretary.

item.title: Young men are flocking to Catholic churches — for faith, community and girlfriends
item.description: Catholic Mass has become New York’s new hot spot for men in their 20s and 30s who are driven by Gen Z influencers and the eternal search for companionship.

item.title: Oil prices jump after Trump’s speech urging other nations to ‘take the lead’ on reopening Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The price of crude surged around 8 percent Thursday morning and stock markets fell after President Donald Trump in his address Wednesday evening presented no plan to open the critical waterway.

item.title: Trump’s hall-of-mirrors speech on Iran
item.description: The president perpetuated the dissonance that has characterized the Iran war from the start.
